text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer. It makes your code readable for both yourself and others. A key practice is using clear naming conventions; choose variable and function names that describe their purpose, like `calculateTotalPrice` instead of just `calc`. Keep your functions short and focused on one specific task. This helps with maintainability, as it becomes easier to debug or update your software without breaking other parts. By following these habits, you create code that is not only correct but also a pleasure to work with in the long run.
